devices.name: Nyanbox
devices.description: Handheld ESP32-based pentesting tool. ESP32 WROOM-32U dual-core 240MHz, WiFi b/g/n, BLE 4.2, three NRF24 radios, 0.96" OLED (128x64 SSD1306), USB-C, 2500mAh battery. Fork of CiferTech's nRFBOX.

firmware.name: nyanBOX
firmware.notes: Canonical upstream by jbohack + zr_crackiin. Ships from main branch only — repo has no tagged releases.

firmware.name: nyanBEE
firmware.notes: ck42x firmware — 54 tools across WiFi/BLE/RF/recon, 22 exclusive to Nyanbee. Bee-themed Mission Mode + gamification. Free binary; source private during active development. SHA-256 verification published.

devices.name: Flipper Zero
devices.description: Portable multi-tool for pentesters and geeks. Sub-GHz, NFC, RFID, IR, iButton, GPIO, BadUSB.

firmware.name: Official
firmware.notes: Official Flipper firmware. Update cadence has slowed — 1.4.3 was a bugfix to 1.4.2's IR CLI plugin. Still the latest tagged OFW release.

firmware.name: Unleashed
firmware.notes: Most stable community fork — API-compatible with OFW, adds region unlock and protocol expansions. unlshd-089 is a SubGHz hotfix for FAAC SLH decode.

firmware.name: Momentum
firmware.notes: Direct successor to Xtreme by the same dev team. Most feature-rich fork — 80+ apps, full JS SDK, custom asset packs. Flash via momentum-fw.dev Web Updater or Flipper Lab.

firmware.name: RogueMaster
firmware.notes: Bleeding-edge kitchen-sink fork that syncs both OFW and Unleashed. GitHub weekly builds; Patreon daily.

firmware.name: Xtreme
firmware.notes: ARCHIVED 2024-11-19. Dev team forked forward into Momentum. Historical reference only — do not flash.

devices.name: HackRF One
devices.description: Wide-band software-defined radio. 1 MHz to 6 GHz, half-duplex transceiver.

firmware.name: Official HackRF
firmware.notes: Official firmware from Great Scott Gadgets. Fixes mixer frequency lock failures and adds larger SPI flash support for HackRF Pro.

firmware.name: PortaPack Mayhem
firmware.notes: Standalone firmware for HackRF + PortaPack H1/H2/H4/H4M add-on. Fork of unmaintained Havoc. Adds HackRF Pro (PRALINE) support. Nightly builds released almost daily; v2.4.0 is current stable.

devices.name: Proxmark3 RDV4
devices.description: High/low frequency RFID research tool. Read, write, emulate, sniff.

firmware.name: Iceman (RRG)
firmware.notes: De-facto standard Proxmark3 firmware — 'BREAKMEIFYOUCAN!' release. Adds Qt6 client, new `lf relay` command, fixes hf mf/mfp wrbl. Tuned for RDV4 by default.

devices.name: O.MG Cable / Plug / UnBlocker
devices.description: Family of USB implants — looks like a charging cable, AC adapter, or USB blocker. WiFi-controlled BadUSB with payload execution.

firmware.name: O.MG Universal Firmware
firmware.notes: Single universal firmware for all O.MG devices (Cable, Adapter, Plug, UnBlocker). v3.0-251217 fixed BR keymap forward/back slash transposition. The legacy DemonSeed variant is absorbed into this universal firmware.

devices.name: Pwnagotchi
devices.description: Raspberry Pi Zero W-based WiFi handshake collector with a virtual-pet UI.

firmware.name: jayofelony Pwnagotchi
firmware.notes: Current de-facto Pwnagotchi distribution — the original evilsocket project is unmaintained. Adds Trixie RPi OS support, Bluetooth tethering, PiSugar plugin updates. Active 2026 development on main branch but no new tagged release since 2.9.5.4.

devices.name: WiFi Pineapple Mark VII
devices.description: Wireless network auditing platform. Rogue AP, MITM, recon modules.

firmware.name: Official (Hak5 portal)
firmware.notes: Hak5's download portal — page renders client-side, exact current version not machine-readable. On the 2.x line per docs.hak5.org. Check the portal for latest.

devices.name: USB Rubber Ducky Mark II
devices.description: Keystroke injection tool. Looks like a USB drive, executes pre-compiled payloads at superhuman speed.

firmware.name: Mark II (PayloadStudio architecture)
firmware.notes: Mark II (2022) is explicitly flashless — payloads are compiled in Hak5 PayloadStudio rather than firmware-flashed. There is no 'Gen3'. Mark I (2011) and Mark II (2022) are the only generations.

devices.name: Bash Bunny Mark II
devices.description: Multi-vector USB attack platform. HID, mass storage, ethernet, serial — all at once.

firmware.name: Official (Hak5 portal)
firmware.notes: Mark II ships with 1.7 from the factory. Latest firmware lives on the Hak5 download portal — page renders client-side, current version not machine-readable. NEVER DOWNGRADE — will brick.

devices.name: M5Stack Cardputer
devices.description: Credit-card-sized ESP32-S3 computer with QWERTY keyboard. Popular target for pentesting firmwares.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Most feature-rich Cardputer firmware — CC1101/NRF24/FM/PN532/BadUSB/RFID/WiFi/BLE. Full hardware support (microphone, RGB LED). Also runs on M5StickC PLUS/PLUS2, Core/Core2/CoreS3, plus Lilygo T-series.

firmware.name: Nemo
firmware.notes: Defensive/prank-focused firmware. Targets Cardputer/ADV, StickCPlus2, StickS3. Includes BadUSB Hunter for testing cables against BadUSB attacks.

devices.name: ESP32 Marauder
devices.description: WiFi + BLE pentesting firmware for ESP32 boards. Deauth, evil portal, sniffing, scanning. Many compatible boards.

firmware.name: ESP32 Marauder (official)
firmware.notes: Canonical WiFi/Bluetooth offensive+defensive ESP32 firmware. v1.12.1 adds Flock advertising rework and channel hop for Flock Sniff. Flash via FZEE Flasher (board-aware) or ESP Terminator; Spacehuhn Web Updater also works with manual binary selection.

firmware.name: Bruce (Marauder-compatible boards)
firmware.notes: Bruce runs on many of the same ESP32-S3/CYD boards as Marauder. Different feature focus (BadUSB, RFID) but overlapping wardriving/deauther toolset.

firmware.name: Ghost ESP
firmware.notes: Pentest-focused ESP32 firmware optimized for screen-based boards, especially Cheap Yellow Display (CYD). Adds tap-to-scroll, numpad AP selection, UI scaling. Public site ghostesp.net.

devices.name: Apex 5 Module
devices.description: ESP32-C5 module for Flipper Zero. Adds 5 GHz WiFi 6, dual Sub-GHz radios, NRF24, and GPS. Announced Feb 2026 at $99. Marauder-family hardware.

firmware.name: Apex 5 Firmware
firmware.notes: Newly announced product. Firmware ships from JustCallMeKoko's Marauder ecosystem. Verify current version on release before flashing — this entry needs re-verification post-launch.

devices.name: M5StickC PLUS2
devices.description: Compact ESP32 wearable with 1.14" color display. Same firmware ecosystem as Cardputer (Bruce, Nemo). Popular for wearable wireless recon.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Same Bruce release as Cardputer — WiFi/BLE/RFID/BadUSB. Targets StickCPlus2 explicitly.

firmware.name: Nemo
firmware.notes: Defensive/prank firmware. Nemo targets StickCPlus2 as a first-class platform.

devices.name: LilyGo T-Embed CC1101
devices.description: ESP32-S3 handheld with built-in CC1101 Sub-GHz radio, rotary encoder, color TFT display. Bruce's flagship target alongside Cardputer.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: T-Embed CC1101 is a flagship Bruce target — first-class CC1101 Sub-GHz support, rotary-encoder UI.

devices.name: Cheap Yellow Display (CYD)
devices.description: $10-15 ESP32 + 2.8" touchscreen. Massive entry-level appeal for the ESP32 pentest ecosystem. Runs Ghost ESP and Bruce. Multiple Chinese vendors sell variants.

firmware.name: Ghost ESP
firmware.notes: Ghost ESP is optimized for CYD specifically — tap-to-scroll, touch-friendly numpad AP selection, UI scaling for the 320x240 display.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce supports CYD as one of its core target platforms.

devices.name: Yard Stick One
devices.description: Half-duplex sub-1 GHz wireless test tool. CC1111-based. Controlled via rfcat from a host. Mature firmware, low update cadence — a staple of the RF cybersec toolkit.

firmware.name: rfcat + CC-Bootloader
firmware.notes: rfcat firmware + Python control library by Atlas. Latest tagged release is v2.0.1 — stable, infrequent updates. Flashed via dfu-util when device is in bootloader mode (hold button on connect).

devices.name: LilyGo T-Dongle S3
devices.description: USB-stick ESP32-S3 with 0.96" ST7735 display, TF slot, and hidden microSD in the USB-A shell. Primary platform for USB Army Knife and popular Marauder target.

firmware.name: USB Army Knife
firmware.notes: HID + mass storage + WiFi/BLE attack surface with web UI. Builds from GitHub Actions (no tagged releases). Flash via ESP Web Tools or project docs; hold BOOT (G0) for download mode.

firmware.name: ESP32 Marauder
firmware.notes: Marauder port for T-Dongle S3 — select board in FZEE Flasher or ESP32 Marauder web updater.

devices.name: M5Stack Cardputer ADV
devices.description: Advanced Cardputer with improved keyboard, microphone, and expansion caps (GPS, CC1101 Sub-GHz). Same Bruce/Nemo ecosystem as the original Cardputer.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Full Bruce feature set on Cardputer ADV — CC1101 cap, PN532, BadUSB, WiFi/BLE offensive tools.

firmware.name: Nemo
firmware.notes: Nemo targets Cardputer ADV alongside StickCPlus2 and original Cardputer.

devices.name: M5Stack Cardputer Zero
devices.description: Pocket Linux handheld (Raspberry Pi CM0) with 46-key keyboard and 1.9" display — not ESP32. Community firmware via M5 on-device store expected post-Kickstarter (May 2026).

firmware.name: M5 official (preorder)
firmware.notes: Hardware launching on Kickstarter May 2026. Firmware distribution model TBD — likely M5 app store + SD images, not WebUSB ESP flashing. Re-verify when units ship.

devices.name: LilyGo T-Deck
devices.description: ESP32-S3 QWERTY handheld with trackball. Bruce for BadUSB/CC1101; Meshtastic is the other major firmware line for this hardware.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce on T-Deck / T-Deck Pro — BadUSB and CC1101; no NRF24 on Deck form factor.

firmware.name: Meshtastic
firmware.notes: Primary firmware for many T-Deck owners — LoRa mesh, not offensive WiFi. Flash via Meshtastic web flasher or device OTA.

devices.name: LilyGo T-Display-S3
devices.description: Compact ESP32-S3 board with 1.9" ST7789 display. Bruce target; common Marauder/CYD-adjacent budget board.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce T-Display-S3 — WiFi/BLE/CC1101 shield support; touchscreen pin conflicts documented upstream.

devices.name: LilyGo T-Embed
devices.description: T-Embed without integrated CC1101 — predecessor to T-Embed CC1101. Bruce-supported with full UI stack.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce on original T-Embed — add CC1101/NRF shields per Bruce wiring docs.

devices.name: LilyGo T-Watch S3
devices.description: Watch-form ESP32-S3. Bruce supports BadUSB; limited RF compared to T-Embed/Cardputer.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce T-Watch S3 — primarily BadUSB + WiFi tooling in wearable form factor.

devices.name: LilyGo T-LoRa Pager
devices.description: Pager-style ESP32-S3 with LoRa. Meshtastic-first hardware; Bruce adds BadUSB path.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce BadUSB + WiFi on T-LoRa Pager.

firmware.name: Meshtastic
firmware.notes: Stock use case for this device — LoRa mesh pager firmware.

devices.name: M5StickC PLUS
devices.description: Older compact M5 stick (ESP32-PICO). Bruce LITE builds available; superseded by PLUS2 but still common.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce supports M5StickC PLUS with LITE_VERSION constraints — check Bruce board matrix.

firmware.name: Nemo
firmware.notes: Nemo lists StickCPlus as supported platform.

devices.name: M5Stack Core2
devices.description: ESP32 touchscreen IoT core. Bruce community target for WiFi/BLE/RF when paired with radio modules.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce on M5Core2 — tone speaker, no RGB; add CC1101/NRF via Grove/shields.

devices.name: M5Stack CoreS3
devices.description: ESP32-S3 flagship M5 core with capacitive touch and camera module support.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce on CoreS3/SE — ESP32-S3 performance; no microphone path on Bruce matrix.

devices.name: Chameleon Ultra
devices.description: Standalone NFC/RFID research and emulation tool. Flash via CLI or MTools BLE; DFU packages on GitHub releases.

firmware.name: Chameleon Ultra FW
firmware.notes: Official Ultra firmware v2.1.0 — Ultralight emulation, hardnested, HIDProx LF, CLI rework. Flash via chameleon CLI or MTools.

devices.name: Chameleon Lite
devices.description: Compact NFC emulation device in the Chameleon family. Same RRG tooling chain as Ultra.

firmware.name: Chameleon Lite FW
firmware.notes: Lite builds ship as lite-dfu-app.zip on the ChameleonUltra release page. Flash via chameleon CLI, Ultra GUI, or MTools BLE.

devices.name: ESP8266 Deauther
devices.description: Classic ESP8266 WiFi deauther boards (DSTIKE Deauther, NodeMCU + OLED, etc.). Original Spacehuhn firmware ecosystem.

firmware.name: esp8266_deauther
firmware.notes: Stable 2.6.1 — project maintenance slowed but still the reference deauther FW. Flash via esptool or deauther web installer builds.

devices.name: Flipper WiFi Dev Board
devices.description: ESP32-S3 companion for Flipper Zero — runs ESP32 Marauder when flashed standalone via USB, or pairs with Flipper apps when docked.

firmware.name: ESP32 Marauder
firmware.notes: Primary community use — Marauder on WiFi Devboard via FZEE or Marauder flasher.

firmware.name: Flipper stock (companion mode)
firmware.notes: When used as Flipper GPIO accessory, update Flipper OFW — no separate ESP flash needed for companion apps.

devices.name: LAN Turtle
devices.description: Covert Ethernet implant running OpenWrt-based Hak5 platform for persistent network access.

firmware.name: Official (Hak5 portal)
firmware.notes: Firmware hosted on Hak5 downloads portal — client-rendered page; verify version in device UI before upgrading.

devices.name: Shark Jack
devices.description: Compact Ethernet attack tool — inline network implants and payload execution.

firmware.name: Official (Hak5 portal)
firmware.notes: Check Hak5 downloads for Shark Jack firmware bundles.

devices.name: Packet Squirrel
devices.description: Inline Ethernet MITM and packet capture appliance from Hak5.

firmware.name: Official (Hak5 portal)
firmware.notes: Firmware via Hak5 download portal.

devices.name: WiFi Duck / WHID Elite
devices.description: WiFi-controlled HID injectors (Hak5 WiFi Duck, WHID Elite clones). Web UI for payload staging over WiFi.

firmware.name: WiFi Duck (Hak5 legacy)
firmware.notes: Hak5 WiFi Duck firmware updates via vendor docs. WHID Elite uses separate WHID ecosystem — not interchangeable.

firmware.name: WHID Elite (community)
firmware.notes: Popular WHID Elite injector — firmware/tools on vendor site, not GitHub releases.

devices.name: RTL-SDR Blog V3
devices.description: USB software-defined radio dongle (receive-only). Used with SDR#, GQRX, URH — not a transmit platform like HackRF.

firmware.name: RTL2832U drivers + software
firmware.notes: No flashable MCU firmware — configure host drivers (Zadig on Windows) and SDR application stack.

devices.name: Ubertooth One
devices.description: 2.4 GHz wireless development platform for Bluetooth monitoring and experimentation.

firmware.name: Ubertooth firmware
firmware.notes: Stable release line — flash via DFU according to GSG docs. Host tools: Kismet, Wireshark plugins.

devices.name: M5StickS3
devices.description: Newer M5 stick form factor (ESP32-S3). Nemo first-class; Bruce support expanding.

firmware.name: Nemo
firmware.notes: Nemo lists StickS3 as supported target.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Check Bruce board matrix for StickS3 — same generation as PLUS2/Cardputer.

devices.name: Proxmark3 Easy / Lite
devices.description: Budget Proxmark3 clones (Easy, Lite, V4). Often ship with outdated FW — flash Iceman for full feature parity with RDV4.

firmware.name: Iceman (RRG)
firmware.notes: Same Iceman build as RDV4 — verify antenna/flash size for clone hardware before flashing.

devices.name: WiFi Pineapple Pager / MK7C
devices.description: Portable WiFi Pineapple form factor (Pager / MK7C line). Hak5 ecosystem firmware.

firmware.name: Official (Hak5 portal)
firmware.notes: Pineapple firmware via Hak5 downloads — same portal family as MK7.

devices.name: houndBEE (CYD)
devices.description: CK42X-native Cheap Yellow Display firmware (MIT fork of ESP32-DIV + nyanBEE modules). Alpha — not flashable yet.

firmware.name: houndBEE-CYD
firmware.notes: Phase 0 scaffolding only — no binaries. Watch ck42x.com/houndbee for first flashable release.

devices.name: M5Stack Core BASIC
devices.description: Original M5Stack Core (ESP32) with 2" TFT and Grove port. Bruce community target when paired with CC1101/NRF24 shields.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce on M5Core BASIC — tone speaker, BadUSB¹ via USB; add RF modules per Bruce wiring docs.

devices.name: Bruce RF Reaper
devices.description: Official Bruce open-source PCB — ESP32-S3 with CC1101, NRF24, fuel gauge, and RGB. Designed for Bruce firmware out of the box.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Flagship Bruce OSS board — full RF feature matrix on bruce.computer/boards. Order PCBs via Elecrow/PCBWay links on site.

devices.name: Bruce PCB V2 (Smoochiee)
devices.description: Community Bruce PCB V2 by Smoochiee — RF exploration board with CC1101, NRF24, PN532 path, and extensive GPIO.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Smoochiee V2 in Bruce board matrix — same flasher flow as other ESP32-S3 Bruce targets.

devices.name: ChameleonMini
devices.description: Classic contactless smartcard emulator (NFC). AVR-based; distinct from Chameleon Ultra/Lite NRF52 line. RRG maintains a community fork.

firmware.name: RRG ChameleonMini
firmware.notes: RRG fork of KAOS ChameleonMini. Infrequent tagged builds — verify latest release before flashing. CLI via chameleonmini project docs.

devices.name: GreatFET One
devices.description: USB peripheral multitool — Facedancer USB attacks, logic analyzer, SPI/I2C bridge. Companion to HackRF in GSG ecosystem.

firmware.name: GreatFET firmware
firmware.notes: Official GSG release v2026.0.0. Flash via DFU / greatfet tooling per wiki. Host tools: Facedancer, libgreat.

devices.name: bladeRF
devices.description: USB 3.0 SDR platform (bladeRF x40/x115, micro, 2.0 micro xA4/xA9). FPGA-hosted signal processing; distinct from HackRF use cases.

firmware.name: bladeRF FPGA + FX3
firmware.notes: Nuand host + FPGA bitstream releases. Flash via bladeRF-cli / libbladeRF documentation for your hardware revision.

devices.name: Key Croc
devices.description: Inline keyboard implant — logs keystrokes and runs payloads when deployed between keyboard and target.

firmware.name: Official (Hak5 portal)
firmware.notes: Key Croc firmware distributed via Hak5 downloads portal. Verify version on device before upgrade.

devices.name: M5Dial
devices.description: ESP32-S3 round display with rotary encoder and touch. Growing Bruce community target for compact UI pentest tools.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Check Bruce release notes for M5Dial support — flash via bruce.computer flasher when listed in board picker.

devices.name: M5Stack AtomS3
devices.description: Tiny ESP32-S3 module with LED matrix. Popular for badges; Marauder ports exist for some Atom form factors.

firmware.name: ESP32 Marauder
firmware.notes: Some Marauder community builds target AtomS3 — confirm board variant in FZEE Flasher before flashing.

devices.name: ESP32-C5 DevKitC-1
devices.description: Espressif ESP32-C5 dev kit (Wi-Fi 6 / BLE). Bruce adds C5 support for early adopters — check matrix for feature limits.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: Bruce ESP32-C5 row — WiFi/BLE/RF features per BruceDevices board matrix; no BadUSB on C5.

devices.name: LilyGo T-Embed CC1101 Plus
devices.description: Updated T-Embed CC1101 with NRF24 and refined RF layout. Bruce first-class target alongside original T-Embed CC1101.

firmware.name: Bruce
firmware.notes: T-Embed CC1101 Plus — full CC1101 + NRF24 in Bruce 1.15 board matrix.

devices.name: LilyGo T-Beam Supreme
devices.description: ESP32-S3 + SX1262 LoRa + GPS flagship for Meshtastic. Not a WiFi offensive board — mesh firmware is the primary path.

firmware.name: Meshtastic
firmware.notes: Primary firmware for T-Beam Supreme — flash via flasher.meshtastic.org and select board target.

devices.name: Waveshare ESP32-S3 1.47"
devices.description: USB-dongle ESP32-S3 with 1.47" round display. USB Army Knife tested hardware — similar class to T-Dongle S3.

firmware.name: USB Army Knife
firmware.notes: Documented USB Army Knife compatible board. Build from GitHub Actions artifacts per project README.

devices.name: Pocket Dongle S3
devices.description: T-Dongle S3–style clone with different SD/button layout. USB Army Knife supports with slower SD — verify hardware before flash.

firmware.name: USB Army Knife
firmware.notes: Listed in USB Army Knife hardware table as Pocket-Dongle-S3. Use Generic board profile if autodetect fails.

devices.name: DSTIKE Deauther (boards)
devices.description: Commercial ESP8266 deauther boards (Deauther Monster, Watch, Pro, etc.) running the Spacehuhn esp8266_deauther firmware family.

firmware.name: esp8266_deauther
firmware.notes: Same firmware as generic ESP8266 Deauther entry — DSTIKE boards ship pre-flashed but often outdated. Re-flash via deauther.com or esptool.

devices.name: Flipper Zero NFC Module
devices.description: Official Flipper NFC expansion module. Firmware ships with Flipper OFW — update the Flipper, not the module in isolation.

firmware.name: Flipper OFW (includes NFC stack)
firmware.notes: NFC features driven by main Flipper firmware. Use lab.flipper.net or qFlipper to update.

devices.name: Flipper Zero Sub-GHz Module
devices.description: External Sub-GHz module for Flipper Zero (CC1101). Hardware firmware bundled with Flipper OFW / community forks.

firmware.name: Flipper OFW
firmware.notes: Sub-GHz stack updated via Flipper firmware. Community forks (Unleashed, Momentum) add regional/protocol patches.

firmware.name: Unleashed
firmware.notes: Region unlock and extended Sub-GHz protocols — flash entire Flipper, module follows.

devices.name: Heltec WiFi LoRa 32 (V3)
devices.description: Popular ESP32-S3 + SX1262 LoRa board. Meshtastic community standard; not a Bruce/Marauder primary target.

firmware.name: Meshtastic
firmware.notes: Heltec V3 is a common Meshtastic target — select exact board in Meshtastic flasher.
